methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Chariot Defense is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $41M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers, with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $41M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $82M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the three same-stage defense peers at $300M, with observed range of $150M to $500M. This implies $300M.

The triangulated valuation range spans $70M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $191M. The wide dispersion reflects two material uncertainties: first, the stage-progression method significantly underweights relative to peer comps, suggesting either Chariot's capital efficiency or lower market positioning versus named peers; second, the three-peer sample, while same-stage and sector-matched, exhibits 3.3x spread between min and max. The analysis is most confident in a $150M–$300M range, bracketed by Ares and Vatn/Stark comparables. Key to narrowing valuation will be understanding Chariot's capital efficiency, customer traction, and defensibility relative to the peer set.
